description_long: This is a double-headed axe with a robust, angular design, featuring two wide, symmetrical blades extending from a central red metallic hub. The blades are silver-gray, each with a slightly curved cutting edge and reinforced with visible rivets along the mounting points. The handle is straight, made of a light brown wood, and capped at both ends with red metallic fittings that match the central hub. The lower end of the handle has a hexagonal red guard, likely for grip and protection, while the upper end is securely embedded into the central hub. The overall construction suggests durability and a focus on both function and visual impact, with clean geometric lines and a stylized, almost fantasy-inspired aesthetic. No visible markings or brand labels are present on the axe.
description: A double-headed axe with silver-gray blades, a straight wooden handle, and red metallic fittings, featuring a bold, angular design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades, the central red hub, and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the layered, angular construction of the red hub and handle fittings.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back faces of the blades and the handle's alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and the geometric red fittings along the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a hexagonal cross-section, capped with red metallic fittings, no visible curvature, and a protective lower guard.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, wide, symmetrical blades with slightly curved edges, reinforced with rivets, and a geometric, angular central hub.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible rivets securing the blades to the central hub.
